data.description: Display/heading text in various places on the site is too large at mobile widths, causing some text to clip or wrap awkwardly. Scope: (1) audit display-text uses across the site at 375px viewport (home hero, section headings, page titles, etc.); (2) reduce the mobile-side of the responsive type scale for the display tier so it fits without truncation; (3) preserve the desktop sizing — only the mobile breakpoint changes. Check both the Tailwind typography config (or globals.css) and any inline text-display-* utility classes. Acceptance: no display text clips, overflows the viewport, or wraps in a way that breaks the design at 375px width. Mobile-first non-negotiable: this is the primary user experience.
data.title: Display text too large on mobile — getting cut off

data.body: Review pass. Diff matches plan exactly: three files changed (src/index.css line 188, src/components/house/HouseBannerGrid.tsx line 22, DESIGN.md line 239), each swapping text-5xl -> text-4xl while preserving md:text-7xl. No collateral edits — src/index.css change is scoped to the .text-display utility only (untouched: .text-title, .text-subtitle, surrounding rules). DESIGN.md edit is confined to the Display tier table row. git grep on the feature branch for 'text-5xl md:text-7xl' across src/ and DESIGN.md returns zero hits, confirming no missed display-tier callsites. pnpm typecheck passes. Desktop md: step is preserved on all three surfaces per mobile-first constraint. Visual validation at 320/375/414/768/1280 is the human walk-through per the plan.
